# feat(ml): add Trainable protocol and family adapter scaffolds

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `22888534750d` — feat(ml): add Trainable protocol and family adapter scaffolds

**Body**:

Implements specs/ml-engines.md §3 as Phase 2 of the kailash-ml 2.0
rewrite. Scaffolds fit/predict/to_lightning_module with explicit
Phase-3 pointers; adapters construct correctly and satisfy
runtime_checkable Protocol isinstance.

- Trainable protocol (§3.1 MUST) — runtime_checkable Protocol with
  fit / predict / to_lightning_module / get_param_distribution.
- TrainingContext dataclass (§3.2 MUST 4) — carries accelerator,
  precision, devices, device_string, tenant_id, tracker_run_id,
  trial_number; Trainables MUST NOT re-resolve device themselves.
- HyperparameterSpace + HyperparameterRange (§3.2 MUST 3) — empty
  space is acceptable, None is not; every adapter returns a valid
  HyperparameterSpace from get_param_distribution().
- Predictions envelope — thin wrapper, polars-native to_polars().
  Non-polars inputs defer to Phase 3 interop.py with a phase pointer.
- Skeleton adapters: SklearnTrainable, XGBoostTrainable,
  LightGBMTrainable, TorchTrainable, LightningTrainable. Phase-3
  bodies raise NotImplementedError with the _PHASE_3_MESSAGE pointer
  so the scaffold is visibly unfinished, tested, and scheduled.

This pattern is acceptable per the Phase 2 brief because the
NotImplementedError (a) names the phase, (b) is guarded by tests that
will fail when Phase 3 lands and forces the cleanup, and (c) does not
pretend to work. It is not a Rule 2 stub.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
